word: H6342 - פָּחַד - pâchad - paw-kkad' - Be afraid

sections.paragraph:
Original: פּחד

Transliteration: pâchad

Phonetic: paw-kkad'

BDB Definition:

  1. to fear, tremble, revere, dread, be in awe or dread
    1. (Qal)
      1. to be in dread
      2. to be in awe
    2. (Piel) to be in great dread
    3. (Hiphil) to cause to dread

Origin: a primitive root

TWOT entry: 1756

Part(s) of speech: Verb
